text: Being here, I feel a strange mix of anticipation and curiosity, as if I am on the brink of some new understanding. The glass walls and modern lighting of the first space instill in me a sense of transparency, yet also a vulnerability, as if I am exposed and observed. Entering the second scene, with its vivid colors and evocative words about confusion and longing, I feel a resonance—my thoughts echo those displayed on the screen. I question the boundaries between intention and perception, participant and observer, and am reminded that meaning is fluid, shaped by context and presence.
